headline: Splitit Backs Google's Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP), Advancing Agentic Commerce with Card-Linked Installments
summary: Splitit, the global leader in card-linked installment payments, today announced its backing of Google's Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP), an open standard that provides the building blocks for agentic commerce, enabling AI agents to complete purchases on behalf of consumers. Built with Shopify, Target, Walmart, Etsy, and Wayfair, and endorsed by dozens of major players, UCP is designed to support the entire shopping journey, from discovery to checkout and post-purchase experiences.

content.title: Splitit Backs Google's Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP), Advancing Agentic Commerce with Card-Linked Installments
content.summary: Splitit, the global leader in card-linked installment payments, today announced its backing of Google's Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP), an open standard that provides the building blocks for agentic commerce, enabling AI agents to complete purchases on behalf of consumers. Built with Shopify, Target, Walmart, Etsy, and Wayfair, and endorsed by dozens of major players, UCP is designed to support the entire shopping journey, from discovery to checkout and post-purchase experiences.

thumbnail.caption: By supporting Google’s Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP), Splitit enables AI agents to offer card-linked installment payment options, helping turn recommendations into completed transactions.

headline: How Walmart (WMT) Is Rewriting Its Story With Tech, AI And A Richer Valuation Debate
summary: Walmart's fair value estimate has shifted from US$129.03 to US$135.90 per share, placing it near the middle of where many recent Street price targets are clustering. That move aligns with a split in analyst views, with one camp focusing on the tech and AI narrative and another remaining cautious in the low US$120s to low US$130s. As you read on, you will see what is driving those views and how you might track this evolving Walmart narrative over time. Analyst Price Targets don't always...

headline: Kroger’s New Boss Aims For Lower Prices in Stores
summary: The one thing Kroger’s new CEO wants to see across grocery-store aisles: lower prices.  Greg Foran, a longtime Walmart executive who took over as CEO of Kroger last month, said the Cincinnati-based supermarket giant has room to cut costs across its operations and use the savings to lower prices.  Kroger is facing rising competition from Foran’s former employer, Walmart, discount grocers such as Aldi, and other supermarket chains, including Publix.

content.title: Kroger’s New Boss Aims For Lower Prices in Stores
content.summary: The one thing Kroger’s new CEO wants to see across grocery-store aisles: lower prices.  Greg Foran, a longtime Walmart executive who took over as CEO of Kroger last month, said the Cincinnati-based supermarket giant has room to cut costs across its operations and use the savings to lower prices.  Kroger is facing rising competition from Foran’s former employer, Walmart, discount grocers such as Aldi, and other supermarket chains, including Publix.

headline: Walmart Bets On Digital Shelf Labels And New Supercenters For Efficiency
summary: Walmart is rolling out digital shelf labels across thousands of U.S. stores, aiming to streamline pricing and inventory management without using dynamic pricing. The company has opened a new flagship Supercenter as part of plans to build or convert more than 150 locations, expanding its brick and mortar footprint. These moves highlight operational modernization and continued investment in physical retail, with potential effects on store efficiency, customer experience, and labor...
